mnemonics: Interface is like a blueprint for a house
Default Methods are like a default set of tools
keyPoints: An interface is a abstract class that cannot be instantiated. It defines a contract that must be implemented by any class that implements it.
A default method in an interface is a method that has a body. It can be overridden by classes implementing the interface.
Static methods in an interface are methods that belong to the interface itself, not to any class implementing it.
A private method in an interface is not accessible from outside the interface. It's used to hide implementation details.
